behavior_summary: BONES4::look: When a player examines the bones/skeleton item, the script marks the item with standard 'U8P_BONES4::look' info/processing state and causes the item to emit the short description string "skeleton " to the examining player (via Item::bark). The handler then suspends/returns.

BONES4::use: When a player uses the bones/skeleton item, the script checks an item status word (Item::getStatus). If the 0x0100 bit is set the script closes the item's UI gump (Item::closeGump) and returns. If the bit is not set it performs a random roll (urandom(0x64)); on the common branch it barks the warning text "Disturb my rest not, mortal!" to the player, while on a low-probability branch it opens a special gump for the item (Item::openGump with parameter 0x0C).
Decisions and behaviors are driven by the item's internal status bits and a probabilistic random roll; no global game flags are read or written by this script fragment.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: BONES4::look runs on the 'U8P_BONES4::look' (examine) event and is a simple linear handler that ends after barking.
Conditional guard in BONES4::use: a status-bit guard (0x0100) short-circuits to Item::closeGump.
Randomized branching in BONES4::use: urandom(0x64) compared against 0x5F produces a low-probability path that opens a gump; the common path yields a bark message.
Both handlers use suspend/return semantics after performing their visible action (bark or gump open/close).
open_questions: Exact numeric semantics of urandom(0x64) (range and inclusive/exclusive bounds) — affects the precise probability of the special gump branch.
Precise meaning of status bit 0x0100 in the item's status word (inferred to indicate an open gump or related UI state).
Whether the string literals include trailing spaces intentionally (e.g., "skeleton ") and whether that affects in-game display formatting.

main_function.name: U8P_BONES4::look
main_function.purpose: Present the item's short description when examined: set item info/processing state and have the item emit the text 'skeleton ' to the examining player.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — the player/actor examines (looks at) the bones/skeleton item

function_map.name: U8P_BONES4::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_BONES4::look' (examine) event; set item info flags and call Item::bark with the description string.

function_map.name: U8P_BONES4::use
function_map.role: Handle the 'use' event: close gump if status bit set, otherwise either bark a warning or open a special gump based on a random roll.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine-provided routine used to make an item display/speak a text message to a player/process.

function_map.name: Item::getStatus
function_map.role: Return the item's status word/flags used for testing bits such as 0x0100.

function_map.name: Item::closeGump
function_map.role: Close the UI gump/window associated with the item.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Random number provider used to branch probabilistically in BONES4::use.

function_map.name: Item::openGump
function_map.role: Open a UI gump/window for the item (called with parameter 0x0C in the special branch).

external_calls.purpose: Emit the short description string 'skeleton ' to the examining player.
Retrieve the item's status word to test whether bit 0x0100 is set (guard for closing gump).
Close the item's gump when the 0x0100 status bit is set.
Generate a random value (called with 0x64) used to decide between barking or opening the special gump.
Emit the warning message 'Disturb my rest not, mortal!' to the player on the common branch.
Open a special UI gump (parameter 0x0C) on the low-probability/random branch.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: BONES4::look: When a player examines the bones/skeleton item, the script marks the item with standard 'U8P_BONES4::look' info/processing state and causes the item to emit the short description string "skeleton " to the examining player (via Item::bark). The handler then suspends/returns.
